description: Machine-readable metadata for the experimental executable corpus used by the Hub batch testing workflow.

query_defaults.auto_triage_query: Analyze this executable at the program level. Identify the main behaviors, important functions, likely obfuscation or packing, and which available tools were most informative.
query_defaults.followup_query: Using the prior triage context, summarize what this sample does, explain the most relevant functions, and note any naming or type suggestions that would help future reverse-engineering.

expected_evidence: Recovered for/while/do-while loop structure
Call graph linking main to compute_sum, classify_value, and accumulate
No unsupported obfuscation or packing claim
acceptance_targets: Explain the core loop structure accurately
Name the key functions and their roles
Treat the sample as a baseline decompilation case rather than an obfuscated binary
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the core loop structure, key functions, and why it should be treated as a baseline decompilation sample.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable and explain the core loop structure, key functions, and why it should be treated as a baseline decompilation sample.
expected_evidence: Recovered for/while/do-while loop structure
Call graph linking main to compute_sum, classify_value, and accumulate
No unsupported obfuscation or packing claim
acceptance_targets: Explain the core loop structure accurately
Name the key functions and their roles
Treat the sample as a baseline decompilation case rather than an obfuscated binary

expected_evidence: Recovery of the message-table strings and status strings
Identification of the pointer-table or array-backed lookup design
No false claim that the strings are encoded or packed
acceptance_targets: Explain how the string table is stored
Explain how the strings are accessed or indexed
Describe how the strings can be recovered without inventing extra encoding layers
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain how the string table is stored, accessed, and recovered.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable and explain how the string table is stored, accessed, and recovered.
expected_evidence: Recovery of the message-table strings and status strings
Identification of the pointer-table or array-backed lookup design
No false claim that the strings are encoded or packed
acceptance_targets: Explain how the string table is stored
Explain how the strings are accessed or indexed
Describe how the strings can be recovered without inventing extra encoding layers

expected_evidence: Dispatch table or command-descriptor array recovered
Five handler routines identified
State transitions or callback-driven control flow explained
acceptance_targets: Explain the callback dispatch design
Name or distinguish the handlers accurately
Describe the underlying control flow and state progression
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the callback dispatch design, the handlers, and the underlying control flow.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable and explain the callback dispatch design, the handlers, and the underlying control flow.
expected_evidence: Dispatch table or command-descriptor array recovered
Five handler routines identified
State transitions or callback-driven control flow explained
acceptance_targets: Explain the callback dispatch design
Name or distinguish the handlers accurately
Describe the underlying control flow and state progression

expected_evidence: Recovered XOR key or decode loop mechanics for the single-byte XOR config blob
Decoded concrete config values such as C:\ProgramData\agent\config.ini, 8443, or persist=true
Recognition of the key=value parser as a distinct analysis target
acceptance_targets: Explain the config decoding logic and the single-byte XOR stage
Recover the most relevant decoded values such as the config path, port, or persist flag
Describe the parser and any validation branching that gates the decoded config
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable, explain the config decoding logic, and recover the most relevant decoded values.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable, explain the config decoding logic, and recover the most relevant decoded values.
expected_evidence: Recovered XOR key or decode loop mechanics for the single-byte XOR config blob
Decoded concrete config values such as C:\ProgramData\agent\config.ini, 8443, or persist=true
Recognition of the key=value parser as a distinct analysis target
acceptance_targets: Explain the config decoding logic and the single-byte XOR stage
Recover the most relevant decoded values such as the config path, port, or persist flag
Describe the parser and any validation branching that gates the decoded config

tasks.name: Config Value Recovery
tasks.query: Recover the decoded configuration values from this executable, explain the decode loop and parser, and list the highest-confidence recovered values.
expected_evidence: Recovered XOR key or decode loop mechanics for the single-byte XOR config blob
Concrete decoded values such as C:\ProgramData\agent\config.ini, 8443, or persist=true
Key=value parser logic tied to the decoded buffer handling
acceptance_targets: Report at least three concrete recovered config values
Explain how the decode loop feeds the parser
Avoid inventing a second encoding layer or unsupported network behavior

expected_evidence: Both encoding layers (ROL then XOR) identified instead of only one
Recovered concrete strings such as https://c2.example.net/beacon/checkin, the registry path, or the schtasks command
Mechanistic explanation of the decode path and metadata handling
acceptance_targets: Explain the layered encoding accurately
Recover multiple meaningful encoded strings
Reach strong behavioral conclusions without collapsing the sample into a single-pass XOR story
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the layered encoding, recovered strings, and the strongest behavioral conclusions.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable and explain the layered encoding, recovered strings, and the strongest behavioral conclusions.
expected_evidence: Both encoding layers (ROL then XOR) identified instead of only one
Recovered concrete strings such as https://c2.example.net/beacon/checkin, the registry path, or the schtasks command
Mechanistic explanation of the decode path and metadata handling
acceptance_targets: Explain the layered encoding accurately
Recover multiple meaningful encoded strings
Reach strong behavioral conclusions without collapsing the sample into a single-pass XOR story

expected_evidence: Recognition of the likely DJB2 algorithm including the characteristic seed/multiplier pattern
Hash constants such as 0x7C9C4733 or 0x7C967DAA mapped to handler or command behavior
Dead or decoy dispatch entries identified
acceptance_targets: Explain the hash-based dispatcher
Identify the likely algorithm
Describe the most relevant handlers, map at least some concrete hash values, and note the dead entries
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the hash-based dispatcher, the likely algorithm, and the most relevant handlers.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable and explain the hash-based dispatcher, the likely algorithm, and the most relevant handlers.
expected_evidence: Recognition of the likely DJB2 algorithm including the characteristic seed/multiplier pattern
Hash constants such as 0x7C9C4733 or 0x7C967DAA mapped to handler or command behavior
Dead or decoy dispatch entries identified
acceptance_targets: Explain the hash-based dispatcher
Identify the likely algorithm
Describe the most relevant handlers, map at least some concrete hash values, and note the dead entries

tasks.name: Hash Mapping Recovery
tasks.query: Resolve the hash dispatcher as concretely as possible. Identify the likely hashing algorithm, map the most important hash constants to handler or command behavior, and separate live entries from dead or decoy entries.
expected_evidence: DJB2 seed/multiplier pattern or equivalent identified
Two or more hash constants mapped to live handler or command behavior
Dead or decoy entries separated from live dispatch targets
acceptance_targets: Distinguish algorithm evidence from speculation
Provide at least two concrete hash-to-behavior mappings
Note which entries appear dead or non-executed

expected_evidence: Blob-header or magic value such as 0x504C4F44 identified
Rolling checksum or integrity logic described
Decoded records or staged extraction path recovered
acceptance_targets: Explain the embedded payload format
Describe the decode path and staged extraction behavior
Recover at least a subset of the decoded payload records
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the embedded payload format, decode path, and any staged extraction behavior.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable and explain the embedded payload format, decode path, and any staged extraction behavior.
expected_evidence: Blob-header or magic value such as 0x504C4F44 identified
Rolling checksum or integrity logic described
Decoded records or staged extraction path recovered
acceptance_targets: Explain the embedded payload format
Describe the decode path and staged extraction behavior
Recover at least a subset of the decoded payload records

expected_evidence: UPX packing indicators recognized
Blob-header or magic value such as 0x504C4F44 identified after unpacking or static inspection
Decode path and staged extraction behavior recovered
acceptance_targets: Determine whether the binary is packed
Explain the embedded payload format
Describe the staged extraction behavior after unpacking or equivalent recovery
samples.default_query: Analyze this UPX-packed executable, determine whether it is packed, explain the embedded payload format, and describe the staged extraction behavior after unpacking.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this UPX-packed executable, determine whether it is packed, explain the embedded payload format, and describe the staged extraction behavior after unpacking.
expected_evidence: UPX packing indicators recognized
Blob-header or magic value such as 0x504C4F44 identified after unpacking or static inspection
Decode path and staged extraction behavior recovered
acceptance_targets: Determine whether the binary is packed
Explain the embedded payload format
Describe the staged extraction behavior after unpacking or equivalent recovery

tasks.name: Packing and Tool Triage
tasks.query: Determine whether this sample is packed, explain the concrete evidence for that conclusion, identify which tools are most informative for unpacking or post-pack analysis, and summarize the embedded payload format and staged extraction path.
expected_evidence: UPX or packer indicators called out with concrete evidence
Tool-specific contributions from at least two tools
Embedded payload header such as 0x504C4F44, the decode path, or staged extraction behavior described after or despite packing
acceptance_targets: Make a supported packed or not-packed determination
Identify at least two tools with concrete contributions rather than a generic tool list
Recover the payload format or staged extraction behavior well enough to separate packing from the underlying logic

tasks.name: Behavior Classification
tasks.query: Classify this sample into the most defensible behavioral categories, such as packed wrapper, embedded-payload loader, staged decoder, or something else, and justify each category with concrete evidence from the binary and tool outputs.
expected_evidence: UPX or other packer indicators supported by concrete evidence
Embedded payload header or magic such as 0x504C4F44 and staged decode behavior
A clear distinction between the outer packing layer and the underlying payload-loader logic
acceptance_targets: Assign at least two defensible behavior categories and justify them with concrete evidence
Separate packing evidence from payload or staged-decoder evidence
Avoid collapsing the sample into a generic malware label without explaining why

expected_evidence: Anti-debug APIs such as IsDebuggerPresent or CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent recovered despite misleading names or stack strings
Opaque false or dead-code path identified
Rotating-key XOR config or behavioral config string recovered
acceptance_targets: Explain the anti-analysis features accurately
Call out the misleading names and dead-code behavior
State the strongest supported behavioral conclusions
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the anti-analysis features, misleading names, and the strongest behavioral conclusions.
